rag_rules: Place AGREE assume/guarantee contracts in component type scopes; use implementation scopes for subcomponent bindings and assignments.
Insert annex blocks within the target component or implementation where the contract applies.
generic_rules: AGREE annexes must reside in valid AADL scopes: type annexes for interface behavior, implementation annexes for binding and execution constraints.
Preserve AADL architecture by avoiding structural redesign during annex fusion.
Use only visible identifiers from the current AADL scope in AGREE contracts.
Do not introduce package-qualified names or properties not present in the input model.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Component type contracts define assumptions and guarantees for ports; implementation contracts define bindings and assignments.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type annexes unless they are valid outputs or features.
Do not introduce new package references or AADL properties as AGREE variables without model support.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE validator diagnostics and repair patterns
